title: Add ssh options to podman machine tests
body:
Add options UserKnownHostsFile=/dev/null, StrictHostKeyChecking=no, and CheckHostIP=no to machine tests so we can share known_hosts files avoiding Remote Host Identification errors.

a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.

@cevich how do I run the tests `machine-linux podman fedora-40 rootless host sqlite` to see if the change fixes [this cirrus run](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5066225183621120/html/machine-linux-podman-fedora-40-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html#t--podman-machine-ssh-ssh-to-running-machine-and-check-os-type--1)? (Resolves: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23505)

> @cevich how do I run the tests `machine-linux podman fedora-40 rootless host sqlite` to see if the change fixes [this cirrus run](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/5066225183621120/html/machine-linux-podman-fedora-40-rootless-host-sqlite.log.html#t--podman-machine-ssh-ssh-to-running-machine-and-check-os-type--1)? (Resolves: #23505)

see pkg/machine/e2e/README.md, but given this is a flake you will not see that likely anyway unless you instrument your podman binary to always assign the same ssh port.

> I don't see this as reasonable fix, this should be fixed in the actual machine code. Machine should not use the known hosts file because any user recreating a new VM can face the same issue.

I was not able to reproduce it with podman machine, though. that's why I fixed it here. but I see your reasoning, makes sense in this regard. I assumed machine code is valid so I thought about the testing env only

still, some of the needed args seem to be used here already
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/ssh.go#L117-L119

> still, some of the needed args seem to be used here already https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/pkg/machine/ssh.go#L117-L119

yeah adding this there seems fine, although /dev/null would nto work on windows but reading online NUL seems to work:  https://superuser.com/questions/19563/how-do-i-skip-the-known-host-question-the-first-time-i-connect-to-a-machine-vi

In go you could juts get the string via `os.DevNull` which should resolve to the right thing based on the current OS.

> In go you could juts get the string via os.DevNull which should resolve to the right thing based on the current OS.

I was just struggling with this :) thanks @Luap99 

closing in favor of https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/23621
